headline: Migrant worker exploitation affects 2.1 million documented workers
context: Malaysia's 2.1 million documented migrant workers face systemic rights violations including passport confiscation and wage theft, per a 2025 ILO report.

cards.big: 2.1 million documented migrant workers face systemic exploitation in Malaysia
cards.sub: ILO found 68% experienced at least one forced labour indicator

cards.big: Employment Act 1955 amendments in 2022 extended protections to foreign workers
cards.sub: Enforcement remains weak with only 23 prosecutions for worker abuse in 2024

cards.big: US TIP Report placed Malaysia on Tier 2 Watch List for 3 consecutive years
cards.sub: Passport confiscation affects an estimated 1.4 million workers

cards.big: Migrant workers contribute RM48 billion annually to Malaysian GDP
cards.sub: Despite economic contribution, 72% report discrimination in daily life

cards.big: A RM48 billion contribution deserves protection, not exploitation
Government signed MOU with Bangladesh on ethical recruitment in January 2025
